# 📘 第 7 章　Software Initialization and Configuration (软件初始化与配置)

> 📄 **Source pages**: 981–989 | 📁 **File**: `chapter_07.md`
> 🎨 **Format**: 中英对照双语 · 图表原始保留 · 中文背景色灰色 · GitHub Flavored Markdown

---

<a id="sec-7"></a>
<a id="sec-7-0"></a>
## 7. Software Initialization and Configuration § | 7. 软件初始化与配置 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The PCI Express Configuration model supports two Configuration Space access mechanisms:

- PCI-compatible Configuration Access Mechanism (CAM) (see § Section 7.2.1 )
- PCI Express Enhanced Configuration Access Mechanism (ECAM) (see § Section 7.2.2 )

The PCI-compatible mechanism supports 100% binary compatibility with Conventional PCI or later aware operating systems and their corresponding bus enumeration and configuration software.

The enhanced mechanism is provided to increase the size of available Configuration Space and to optimize access mechanisms.

To maintain compatibility with PCI software configuration mechanisms, all PCI Express elements have a PCI-compatible Configuration Space. Each PCI Express Link originates from a logical PCI-PCI Bridge and is mapped into Configuration Space as the secondary bus of this Bridge. The Root Port is a PCI-PCI Bridge structure that originates a PCI Express Link from a PCI Express Root Complex (see § Figure 7-1).

A PCI Express Switch not using FPB Routing ID mechanisms is represented by multiple PCI-PCI Bridge structures connecting PCI Express Links to an internal logical PCI bus (see § Figure 7-2). The Switch Upstream Port is a PCI-PCI Bridge; the secondary bus of this Bridge represents the Switch's internal routing logic. Switch Downstream Ports are PCI-PCI Bridges bridging from the internal bus to buses representing the Downstream PCI Express Links from a PCI Express Switch. Only the PCI-PCI Bridges representing the Switch Downstream Ports may appear on the internal bus. Endpoints, represented by Type 0 Configuration Space Headers, are not permitted to appear on the internal bus.

A PCI Express Endpoint is mapped into Configuration Space as a single Function in a Device, which might contain multiple Functions or just that Function. PCI Express Endpoints and Legacy Endpoints are required to appear within one of the Hierarchy Domains originated by the Root Complex, meaning that they appear in Configuration Space in a tree that has a Root Port as its head. Root Complex Integrated Endpoints (RCiEPs) and Root Complex Event Collectors do not appear within one of the Hierarchy Domains originated by the Root Complex. These appear in Configuration Space as peers of the Root Ports.

Unless otherwise specified, requirements in the Configuration Space definition for a device apply to single Function devices as well as to each Function individually of a Multi-Function Device.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

PCI Express 配置模型支持两种配置空间访问机制:

- PCI 兼容配置访问机制 (CAM) (见 § 第 7.2.1 节)
- PCI Express 增强配置访问机制 (ECAM) (见 § 第 7.2.2 节)

PCI 兼容机制可与早期感知 Conventional PCI 或更高版本的操作系统及其对应的总线枚举与配置软件保持 100% 的二进制兼容。

提供增强机制是为了扩展可用配置空间的容量，并优化访问机制。

为保持与 PCI 软件配置机制的兼容性，所有 PCI Express 元素都具有 PCI 兼容配置空间。每个 PCI Express 链路源自一个逻辑 PCI-PCI 桥，并作为该桥的 secondary 总线映射到配置空间中。根端口 (Root Port) 是一个 PCI-PCI 桥结构，用于从 PCI Express 根复合体 (Root Complex) 引出一条 PCI Express 链路 (见 § 图 7-1)。

不使用 FPB Routing ID 机制的 PCI Express 交换机 (Switch) 由多个 PCI-PCI 桥结构表示，这些桥结构将 PCI Express 链路连接到一个内部逻辑 PCI 总线 (见 § 图 7-2)。交换机上游端口 (Upstream Port) 是一个 PCI-PCI 桥;该桥的 secondary 总线表示交换机的内部路由逻辑。交换机下游端口 (Downstream Port) 是从内部总线桥接到表示来自 PCI Express 交换机的下游 PCI Express 链路的总线的 PCI-PCI 桥。只有表示交换机下游端口的 PCI-PCI 桥才可以出现在内部总线上。由 Type 0 配置空间头表示的端点 (Endpoint) 不允许出现在内部总线上。

PCI Express 端点 (Endpoint) 在配置空间中映射为设备 (Device) 中的单个 Function (功能)，该设备可能包含多个 Function 或仅包含这一个 Function。PCI Express 端点和传统端点 (Legacy Endpoint) 必须出现在由根复合体启动的某个层级域 (Hierarchy Domain) 内，即它们在配置空间中以根端口为根的树形结构出现。根复合体集成端点 (RCiEP) 和根复合体事件收集器不出现在由根复合体启动的层级域内。它们在配置空间中作为根端口的对等实体出现。

除非另有规定，否则配置空间定义中针对设备的要求既适用于单 Function 设备，也适用于多功能 (Multi-Function) 设备中的每一个 Function。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>

</div>

<!-- 📄 Page 982 -->

<a id="sec-7-2"></a>
## 7.2 PCI Express Configuration Mechanisms § | 7.2 PCI Express 配置机制 §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

PCI Express extends the Configuration Space to 4096 bytes per Function as compared to 256 bytes allowed by [PCI]. PCI Express Configuration Space is divided into a PCI-compatible region, which consists of the first 256 bytes of a Function's Configuration Space, and a PCI Express Extended Configuration Space which consists of the remaining Configuration Space (see § Figure 7-3). The PCI-compatible Configuration Space can be accessed using either the mechanism defined in § Section 7.2.1 or § Section 7.2.2 . Accesses made using either access mechanism are equivalent. The PCI Express Extended Configuration Space can only be accessed by using the ECAM mechanism defined in § Section 7.2.2 .

> **Figure 7-3.** PCI Express Configuration Space Layout
> <img src="figures/chapter_07/fig_0983_1_tight.png" width="700">

The PCI-compatible PCI Express Configuration Mechanism supports the PCI Configuration Space programming model defined in the [PCI]. By adhering to this model, systems incorporating PCI Express interfaces remain compliant with conventional PCI bus enumeration and configuration software.

In the same manner as PCI device Functions, PCI Express device Functions are required to provide a Configuration Space for software-driven initialization and configuration. Except for the differences described in this chapter, the PCI Express Configuration Space headers are organized to correspond with the format and behavior defined in the [PCI] (Section 6.1).

The PCI-compatible Configuration Access Mechanism uses the same Request format as the ECAM. For PCI-compatible Configuration Requests, the Extended Register Address field must be all zeros.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

相对于 [PCI] 所允许的每 Function 256 字节，PCI Express 将配置空间扩展到每 Function 4096 字节。PCI Express 配置空间分为一个 PCI 兼容区域 (由 Function 配置空间的前 256 字节组成) 和一个 PCI Express 扩展配置空间 (由剩余的配置空间组成) (见 § 图 7-3)。PCI 兼容配置空间可以使用 § 第 7.2.1 节或 § 第 7.2.2 节定义的任一机制访问。使用任一访问机制进行的访问是等效的。PCI Express 扩展配置空间只能使用 § 第 7.2.2 节定义的 ECAM 机制访问。

PCI 兼容的 PCI Express 配置机制支持 [PCI] 中定义的 PCI 配置空间编程模型。通过遵循该模型，集成 PCI Express 接口的系统能够保持与传统 PCI 总线枚举和配置软件的兼容性。

与 PCI 设备 Function 一样，PCI Express 设备 Function 也必须提供配置空间，以支持软件驱动的初始化和配置。除本章所述差异外，PCI Express 配置空间头的组织方式与 [PCI] (第 6.1 节) 中定义的格式和行为相对应。

PCI 兼容配置访问机制使用与 ECAM 相同的请求格式。对于 PCI 兼容配置请求，扩展寄存器地址 (Extended Register Address) 字段必须全部为零。

<img src="figures/chapter_07/fig_0983_1_tight.png" width="700">
</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<!-- 📄 Page 983 -->

<a id="sec-7-2-1"></a>
## 7.2.1 PCI-compatible Configuration Mechanism § | 7.2.1 PCI 兼容配置机制 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **CHANGING A DEVICE'S OS DRIVER BINDINGS & RESOURCES**
>
> Under certain circumstances, it is highly useful for a device to change one or more of its architected registers that help determine which OS-managed resources are allocated to the device and which OS drivers get bound to it.
>
> Here are two key examples:
>
> - A device might change the Class Code Register value in one or more of its Functions
> - Entire Functions might be added or removed after device firmware is updated
>
> Software can direct devices to change their architected registers through a variety of mechanisms outside the scope of this specification, including implementation specific ones and ones defined by external standards bodies. It is recommended that these mechanisms follow the guidance of this Implementation Note.
>
> If a device outside the RC has possibly been enumerated by the OS, it is strongly recommended that software use a mechanism directing the device to change its registers when coming out of a Conventional Reset. From a hardware perspective, this is similar to a hot remove followed by a hot add, providing a clean trigger point for architected registers to change in compliance with this specification, plus it guarantees the architected default hardware state for OS I/O infrastructure software and OS drivers to rely on. It is strongly recommended that software use OS-specific interfaces to perform the Conventional Reset and/or coordinate the reset and subsequent enumeration with the OS. If not feasible via OS-specific interfaces, software may be able to perform a Conventional Reset directly via several ways, including the Secondary Bus Reset bit or the Link Disable bit in the Downstream Port above the device.
>
> If software knows that a device outside the RC has not been enumerated by the OS, software may choose to direct the device to change its registers without undergoing a reset, thus avoiding unnecessary delay.

> **Footnote 149.** Future PCI Express Switches may be implemented as a single Switch device component (without the PCI-PCI Bridges) that is not limited by legacy compatibility requirements imposed by existing PCI software.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

> **实现注意事项 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **修改设备的 OS 驱动绑定与资源 (CHANGING A DEVICE'S OS DRIVER BINDINGS & RESOURCES)**
>
> 在某些情况下，允许设备修改其架构寄存器中的一个或多个 (这些寄存器有助于确定分配给设备的 OS 管理资源以及绑定到设备的 OS 驱动) 是非常有益的。
>
> 以下是两个关键示例:
>
> - 设备可能修改其一个或多个 Function 中的 Class Code 寄存器值
> - 在设备固件更新后，可能新增或移除整个 Function
>
> 软件可以通过本规范范围之外的多种机制来指示设备修改其架构寄存器，包括特定于实现的机制以及由外部标准机构定义的机制。建议这些机制遵循本实现注意事项的指导。
>
> 如果 RC 之外的设备可能已被 OS 枚举过，强烈建议软件使用一种机制，在设备退出常规复位 (Conventional Reset) 时指示设备修改其寄存器。从硬件的角度来看，这类似于先热移除再热添加的过程，提供了一个干净的触发点使架构寄存器按照本规范发生改变，并保证为 OS I/O 基础设施软件和 OS 驱动所依赖的架构默认硬件状态。强烈建议软件使用 OS 特定的接口来执行常规复位 (Conventional Reset)，或协调复位与随后的 OS 枚举。如果通过 OS 特定接口不可行，软件也可以通过多种方式直接执行常规复位 (Conventional Reset)，包括使用设备上方下游端口中的 Secondary Bus Reset 位或 Link Disable 位。
>
> 如果软件知道 RC 之外的设备尚未被 OS 枚举，则可以选择在不进行复位的情况下指示设备修改其寄存器，从而避免不必要的延迟。

> **脚注 149.** 未来的 PCI Express 交换机可以实现为单个交换机设备组件 (不含 PCI-PCI 桥)，不受现有 PCI 软件所强加的旧式兼容性要求的限制。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 984 -->
---

<a id="sec-7-2-2"></a>
## 7.2.2 PCI Express Enhanced Configuration Access Mechanism (ECAM) § | 7.2.2 PCI Express 增强配置访问机制 (ECAM) §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For systems that are PC-compatible, or that do not implement a processor-architecture-specific firmware interface standard that allows access to the Configuration Space, the Enhanced Configuration Access Mechanism (ECAM) is required as defined in this section.

For systems that implement a processor-architecture-specific firmware interface standard that allows access to the Configuration Space, the operating system uses the standard firmware interface, and the hardware access mechanism defined in this section is not required.

In all systems, device drivers are encouraged to use the application programming interface (API) provided by the operating system to access the Configuration Space of its device and not directly use the hardware mechanism.

The ECAM utilizes a flat memory-mapped address space to access device configuration registers. In this case, the memory address determines the configuration register accessed and the memory data updates (for a write) or returns the contents of (for a read) the addressed register. The mapping from memory address space to PCI Express Configuration Space address is defined in § Table 7-1.

The size and base address for the range of memory addresses mapped to the Configuration Space are determined by the design of the host bridge and the firmware. They are reported by the firmware to the operating system in an implementation specific manner. The size of the range is determined by the number of bits that the host bridge maps to the Bus Number field in the configuration address. In § Table 7-1, this number of bits is represented as n, where 1 ≤ n ≤ 8.

A host bridge that maps n memory address bits to the Bus Number field supports Bus Numbers from 0 to 2^n -1, inclusive, and the base address of the range is aligned to a 2^(n+20)-byte memory address boundary. Any bits in the Bus Number field that are not mapped from memory address bits must be Clear.

For example, if a system maps three memory address bits to the Bus Number field, the following are all true:

- n = 3.
- Address bits A[63:23] are used for the base address, which is aligned to a 2^23-byte (8 MB) boundary.
- Address bits A[22:20] are mapped to bits [2:0] in the Bus Number field.
- Bits [7:3] in the Bus Number field are set to Clear.
- The system is capable of addressing Bus Numbers between 0 and 7, inclusive.

A minimum of one memory address bit (n = 1) must be mapped to the Bus Number field. Systems are encouraged to map additional memory address bits to the Bus Number field as needed to support a larger number of buses. Systems that support more than 4 GB of memory addresses are encouraged to map eight bits of memory address (n = 8) to the Bus Number field. Note that in systems that include multiple host bridges with different ranges of Bus Numbers assigned to each host bridge, the highest Bus Number for the system is limited by the number of bits mapped by the host bridge to which the highest bus number is assigned. In such a system, the highest Bus Number assigned to a particular host bridge would be greater, in most cases, than the number of buses assigned to that host bridge. In other words, for each host bridge, the number of bits mapped to the Bus Number field, n, must be large enough that the highest Bus Number assigned to each particular bridge must be less than or equal to 2^n -1 for that bridge.

In some processor architectures, it is possible to generate memory accesses that cannot be expressed in a single Configuration Request, for example due to crossing a DW aligned boundary, or because a locked access is used. A Root Complex implementation is not required to support the translation to Configuration Requests of such accesses.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于 PC 兼容的系统，或者未实现允许访问配置空间的处理器架构特定固件接口标准的系统，必须按本节定义实现增强配置访问机制 (ECAM)。

对于实现了允许访问配置空间的处理器架构特定固件接口标准的系统，操作系统使用该标准固件接口，本节定义的硬件访问机制不是必需的。

在所有系统中，鼓励设备驱动使用操作系统提供的应用程序编程接口 (API) 来访问其设备的配置空间，而不是直接使用硬件机制。

ECAM 利用平坦的内存映射地址空间来访问设备配置寄存器。在这种情况下，内存地址决定所访问的配置寄存器，内存数据 (对于写操作) 更新或 (对于读操作) 返回被寻址寄存器的内容。从内存地址空间到 PCI Express 配置空间地址的映射在 § 表 7-1 中定义。

映射到配置空间的内存地址范围的大小和基地址由主桥 (Host Bridge) 的设计和固件决定。它们以特定于实现的方式由固件报告给操作系统。范围的大小由主桥映射到配置地址中 Bus Number 字段的位数决定。在 § 表 7-1 中，此位数表示为 n，其中 1 ≤ n ≤ 8。

将 n 位内存地址映射到 Bus Number 字段的主桥支持 Bus Number 从 0 到 2^n - 1 (含)，并且该范围的基地址对齐到 2^(n+20) 字节的内存地址边界。Bus Number 字段中未从内存地址映射的任何位必须清零 (Clear)。

例如，如果系统将 3 位内存地址映射到 Bus Number 字段，则以下各项均成立:

- n = 3。
- 地址位 A[63:23] 用于基地址，该基地址对齐到 2^23 字节 (8 MB) 边界。
- 地址位 A[22:20] 映射到 Bus Number 字段中的 [2:0] 位。
- Bus Number 字段中的 [7:3] 位被设置为清零 (Clear)。
- 系统能够寻址 0 到 7 (含) 范围内的 Bus Number。

必须至少将一位内存地址 (n = 1) 映射到 Bus Number 字段。鼓励系统根据需要将更多的内存地址位映射到 Bus Number 字段以支持更多的总线数。支持超过 4 GB 内存地址的系统鼓励将 8 位内存地址 (n = 8) 映射到 Bus Number 字段。请注意，在包含多个主桥且为每个主桥分配不同 Bus Number 范围的系统中，系统的最高 Bus Number 受分配最高 Bus Number 的主桥所映射的位数限制。在这种情况下，分配给特定主桥的最高 Bus Number 在大多数情况下会大于分配给该主桥的总线数。换言之，对于每个主桥，映射到 Bus Number 字段的位数 n 必须足够大，使得分配给每个特定桥的最高 Bus Number 必须小于或等于该桥的 2^n - 1。

在某些处理器架构中，可能生成无法在单个配置请求中表达的内存访问，例如跨越 DW 对齐边界，或使用锁定访问。根复合体 (Root Complex) 的实现不需要支持将这些访问转换为配置请求。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 985 -->
---

**Table 7-1. Enhanced Configuration Address Mapping | 表 7-1. 增强配置地址映射**

| Memory Address | PCI Express Configuration Space |
|---|---|
| A[(20+n-1):20] | Bus Number (1 ≤ n ≤ 8) |
| A[19:15] | Device Number |
| A[14:12] | Function Number |
| A[11:8] | Extended Register Number |
| A[7:2] | Register Number |
| A[1:0] | Along with size of the access, used to generate Byte Enables |

> **Note:** for Requests targeting Extended Functions in an ARI Device, A[19:12] represents the (8-bit) Function Number, which replaces the (5-bit) Device Number and (3-bit) Function Number fields above.

> **Footnote 151.** This address refers to the byte-level address from a software point of view.

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The system hardware must provide a method for the system software to guarantee that a write transaction using the ECAM is completed by the completer before system software execution continues.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

系统硬件必须为系统软件提供一种方法，以保证在使用 ECAM 的写事务被完成端 (completer) 完成之后，系统软件才继续执行。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 986 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **ORDERING CONSIDERATIONS FOR THE ENHANCED CONFIGURATION ACCESS MECHANISM**
>
> The ECAM converts memory transactions from the host CPU into Configuration Requests on the PCI Express fabric. This conversion potentially creates ordering problems for the software, because writes to memory addresses are typically posted transactions but writes to Configuration Space are not posted on the PCI Express fabric.
>
> Generally, software does not know when a posted transaction is completed by the completer. In those cases in which the software must know that a posted transaction is completed by the completer, one technique commonly used by the software is to read the location that was just written. For systems that follow the PCI ordering rules throughout, the read transaction will not complete until the posted write is complete. However, since the PCI ordering rules allow non-posted write and read transactions to be reordered with respect to each other, the CPU must wait for a non-posted write to complete on the PCI Express fabric to be guaranteed that the transaction is completed by the completer.
>
> As an example, software may wish to configure a device Function's Base Address register by writing to the device using the ECAM, and then read a location in the memory-mapped range described by this Base Address register. If the software were to issue the memory-mapped read before the ECAM write was completed, it would be possible for the memory-mapped read to be re-ordered and arrive at the device before the Configuration Write Request, thus causing unpredictable results.
>
> To avoid this problem, processor and host bridge implementations must ensure that a method exists for the software to determine when the write using the ECAM is completed by the completer.
>
> This method may simply be that the processor itself recognizes a memory range dedicated for mapping ECAM accesses as unique, and treats accesses to this range in the same manner that it would treat other accesses that generate non-posted writes on the PCI Express fabric, i.e., that the transaction is not posted from the processor's viewpoint. An alternative mechanism is for the host bridge (rather than the processor) to recognize the memory-mapped Configuration Space accesses and not to indicate to the processor that this write has been accepted until the non-posted Configuration Transaction has completed on the PCI Express fabric. A third alternative would be for the processor and host bridge to post the memory-mapped write to the ECAM and for the host bridge to provide a separate register that the software can read to determine when the Configuration Write Request has completed on the PCI Express fabric. Other alternatives are also possible.

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **GENERATING CONFIGURATION REQUESTS**
>
> Because Root Complex implementations are not required to support the generation of Configuration Requests from accesses that cross DW boundaries, or that use locked semantics, software should take care not to cause the generation of such accesses when using the memory-mapped ECAM unless it is known that the Root Complex implementation being used will support the translation.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

> **实现注意事项 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **增强配置访问机制的排序考量 (ORDERING CONSIDERATIONS FOR THE ENHANCED CONFIGURATION ACCESS MECHANISM)**
>
> ECAM 将来自主机 CPU 的内存事务转换为 PCI Express 互连上的配置请求。由于对内存地址的写入通常是 Posted 事务，而对配置空间的写入在 PCI Express 互连上不是 Posted 的，因此这种转换可能会给软件带来排序问题。
>
> 通常，软件并不知道 Posted 事务何时被完成端 (completer) 完成。在软件必须知道 Posted 事务已被完成端完成的场景中，软件常用的一种技术是读取刚刚写入的位置。对于完全遵循 PCI 排序规则的系统，读取事务将在 Posted 写入完成之前不会完成。然而，由于 PCI 排序规则允许非 Posted 写入和读取事务彼此之间重新排序，CPU 必须等待非 Posted 写入在 PCI Express 互连上完成，才能保证该事务已被完成端完成。
>
> 作为一个示例，软件可能希望通过使用 ECAM 向设备写入来配置设备 Function 的基址寄存器 (Base Address Register)，然后读取该基址寄存器所描述的内存映射范围内的某个位置。如果软件在 ECAM 写入完成之前发起内存映射读取，则内存映射读取可能被重新排序，并在配置写入请求 (Configuration Write Request) 之前到达设备，从而导致不可预测的结果。
>
> 为避免此问题，处理器和主桥 (Host Bridge) 的实现必须确保存在某种机制，使软件能够判断使用 ECAM 的写入何时被完成端 (completer) 完成。
>
> 一种简单的方法是处理器自身将专用于映射 ECAM 访问的内存范围识别为唯一范围，并以处理在 PCI Express 互连上生成非 Posted 写入的访问相同的方式处理对该范围的访问，即从处理器的视角来看该事务不是 Posted 的。另一种机制是由主桥 (而非处理器) 识别内存映射的配置空间访问，并且在该非 Posted 配置事务在 PCI Express 互连上完成之前不向处理器指示该写入已被接受。第三种替代方案是处理器和主桥将内存映射写入发布到 ECAM，然后主桥提供一个单独的寄存器，软件可以读取该寄存器以判断配置写入请求 (Configuration Write Request) 何时已在 PCI Express 互连上完成。当然也存在其他替代方案。
>
> **实现注意事项 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **生成配置请求 (GENERATING CONFIGURATION REQUESTS)**
>
> 由于根复合体 (Root Complex) 的实现不需要支持从跨越 DW 边界或使用锁定语义的访问生成配置请求，因此在使用内存映射 ECAM 时，软件应注意避免生成此类访问，除非已知所使用的根复合体实现支持此类转换。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

<!-- 📄 Page 987 -->

<a id="sec-7-2-2-1"></a>
## 7.2.2.1 Host Bridge Requirements § | 7.2.2.1 主桥 (Host Bridge) 要求 §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For those systems that implement the ECAM, the PCI Express Host Bridge is required to translate the memory-mapped PCI Express Configuration Space accesses from the host processor to PCI Express configuration transactions. The use of Host Bridge PCI class code is Reserved for backwards compatibility; Host Bridge Configuration Space is opaque to standard PCI Express software and may be implemented in an implementation specific manner that is compatible with PCI Host Bridge Type 0 Configuration Space. A PCI Express Host Bridge is not required to signal errors through a Root Complex Event Collector. This support is optional for PCI Express Host Bridges.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于实现 ECAM 的系统，PCI Express 主桥 (Host Bridge) 必须将来自主处理器的内存映射 PCI Express 配置空间访问转换为 PCI Express 配置事务。Host Bridge PCI class code 的使用被保留 (Reserved) 以保持向后兼容性;主桥配置空间对标准 PCI Express 软件是不透明的，可以以与 PCI Host Bridge Type 0 配置空间兼容的特定于实现的方式实现。PCI Express 主桥不需要通过根复合体事件收集器上报错误。该支持对 PCI Express 主桥是可选的。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-7-2-2-2"></a>
## 7.2.2.2 PCI Express Device Requirements § | 7.2.2.2 PCI Express 设备要求 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Devices must support an additional 4 bits for decoding configuration register access, i.e., they must decode the Extended Register Address[3:0] field of the Configuration Request header.

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **DEVICE-SPECIFIC REGISTERS IN CONFIGURATION SPACE**
>
> It is strongly recommended that PCI Express devices place no registers in Configuration Space other than those in headers or Capability structures architected by applicable PCI specifications.
>
> Device-specific registers that have legitimate reasons to be placed in Configuration Space (e.g., they need to be accessible before Memory Space is allocated) should be placed in a Vendor-Specific Capability structure (§ Section 7.9.4 ), a Vendor-Specific Extended Capability structure (§ Section 7.9.5 , or § Section 7.9.6 ).
>
> Device-specific registers accessed in the run-time environment by drivers should be placed in Memory Space that is allocated by one or more Base Address registers. Even though PCI-compatible or PCI Express Extended Configuration Space may have adequate room for run-time device-specific registers, placing them there is highly discouraged for the following reasons:
>
> - Not all Operating Systems permit drivers to access Configuration Space directly.
> - Some platforms provide access to Configuration Space only via firmware calls, which typically have substantially lower performance compared to mechanisms for accessing Memory Space.
> - Even on platforms that provide direct access to a memory-mapped PCI Express Enhanced Configuration Mechanism, performance for accessing Configuration Space will typically be significantly lower than for accessing Memory Space since:
>   - Configuration Reads and Writes must usually be DWORD or smaller in size,
>   - Configuration Writes are usually not posted by the platform, and
>   - Some platforms support only one outstanding Configuration Write at a time.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

设备必须额外支持 4 位用于解码配置寄存器访问，即必须解码配置请求头的 Extended Register Address[3:0] 字段。

> **实现注意事项 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **配置空间中的设备特定寄存器 (DEVICE-SPECIFIC REGISTERS IN CONFIGURATION SPACE)**
>
> 强烈建议 PCI Express 设备在配置空间中仅放置由适用 PCI 规范所架构的头或能力结构中的寄存器，不要放置其他寄存器。
>
> 出于合理原因需要放在配置空间中的设备特定寄存器 (例如需要在分配内存空间 (Memory Space) 之前可访问) 应放置在厂商特定能力结构 (§ 第 7.9.4 节)、厂商特定扩展能力结构 (§ 第 7.9.5 节 或 § 第 7.9.6 节) 中。
>
> 驱动在运行时访问的设备特定寄存器应放置在由一个或多个基址寄存器 (BAR) 分配的内存空间 (Memory Space) 中。尽管 PCI 兼容或 PCI Express 扩展配置空间可能为运行时设备特定寄存器提供足够的空间，但出于以下原因，强烈不鼓励将其放在配置空间中:
>
> - 并非所有操作系统都允许驱动直接访问配置空间。
> - 某些平台仅通过固件调用提供对配置空间的访问，与访问内存空间 (Memory Space) 的机制相比，性能通常显著较低。
> - 即使在提供对内存映射 PCI Express 增强配置机制直接访问的平台上，由于以下原因，访问配置空间的性能通常显著低于访问内存空间 (Memory Space):
>   - 配置读写的大小通常必须为 DWORD 或更小，
>   - 配置写入通常不被平台发布 (Posted)，
>   - 某些平台同时仅支持一次未完成的配置写入。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 988 -->
---

<a id="sec-7-2-3"></a>
## 7.2.3 Root Complex Register Block (RCRB) § | 7.2.3 根复合体寄存器块 (RCRB) §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

A Root Port or RCiEP may be associated with an optional 4096-byte block of memory mapped registers referred to as the Root Complex Register Block (RCRB). These registers are used in a manner similar to Configuration Space and can include PCI Express Extended Capabilities and other implementation specific registers that apply to the Root Complex. The structure of the RCRB is described in § Section 7.6.2 .

Multiple Root Ports or internal devices are permitted to be associated with the same RCRB. The RCRB memory-mapped registers must not reside in the same address space as the memory-mapped Configuration Space or Memory Space.

A Root Complex implementation is not required to support accesses to an RCRB that cross DWORD aligned boundaries or accesses that use locked semantics.

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **ACCESSING ROOT COMPLEX REGISTER BLOCK**
>
> Because Root Complex implementations are not required to support accesses to a RCRB that cross DW boundaries, or that use locked semantics, software should take care not to cause the generation of such accesses when accessing a RCRB unless the Root Complex will support the access.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

根端口 (Root Port) 或 RCiEP 可以关联一个可选的 4096 字节内存映射寄存器块，称为根复合体寄存器块 (Root Complex Register Block, RCRB)。这些寄存器的使用方式与配置空间类似，可以包括适用于根复合体的 PCI Express 扩展能力 (Extended Capabilities) 和其他特定于实现的寄存器。RCRB 的结构在 § 第 7.6.2 节中描述。

允许将多个根端口 (Root Port) 或内部设备关联到同一个 RCRB。RCRB 内存映射寄存器不得驻留于与内存映射配置空间或内存空间 (Memory Space) 相同的地址空间中。

根复合体 (Root Complex) 的实现不需要支持对 RCRB 的跨 DWORD 对齐边界的访问或使用锁定语义的访问。

> **实现注意事项 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **访问根复合体寄存器块 (ACCESSING ROOT COMPLEX REGISTER BLOCK)**
>
> 由于根复合体 (Root Complex) 的实现不需要支持对 RCRB 的跨 DW 边界访问或使用锁定语义的访问，因此软件在访问 RCRB 时应注意避免生成此类访问，除非已知该根复合体支持此类访问。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-7-3"></a>
## 7.3 Configuration Transaction Rules § | 7.3 配置事务规则 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

With non-ARI Devices, PCI Express components are restricted to implementing a single Device Number on their primary interface (Upstream Port), but are permitted to implement up to eight independent Functions within that Device Number. Each internal Function is selected based on decoded address information that is provided as part of the address portion of Configuration Request packets.

Except when FPB Routing ID mechanisms are used (see § Section 6.26 ), Downstream Ports that do not have ARI Forwarding enabled must associate only Device 0 with the device attached to the Logical Bus representing the Link from the Port. Configuration Requests targeting the Bus Number associated with a Link specifying Device Number 0 are delivered to the device attached to the Link; Configuration Requests specifying all other Device Numbers (1-31) must be terminated by the Switch Downstream Port or the Root Port with an Unsupported Request Completion Status (equivalent to Master Abort in PCI).

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **CONFIGURATION SPACE READ SIDE EFFECTS**
>
> During a read access, any observable interaction that occurs besides the desired value being returned is called a read side effect. System software that has no specific knowledge of the Function being accessed may issue read requests to anywhere within the Function's Configuration Space. It is highly undesirable that any such access has any read side effects. No such side effects are required in any of the Configuration Space registers defined in this specification. It is strongly recommended that any implementation of those registers, as well as any vendor-defined Configuration Space registers, be free of any read side effects.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于非 ARI 设备，PCI Express 组件在其主要接口 (上游端口 Upstream Port) 上仅限于实现单个设备号 (Device Number)，但允许在该设备号内实现最多八个独立 Function。每个内部 Function 基于配置请求包地址部分所提供的解码地址信息进行选择。

除非使用 FPB Routing ID 机制 (见 § 第 6.26 节)，否则未启用 ARI 转发 (ARI Forwarding) 的下游端口必须仅将设备 0 关联到连接到表示从该端口发出链路的逻辑总线的设备。针对与指定设备号 0 的链路相关联 Bus Number 的配置请求被传递给连接到该链路的设备;指定所有其他设备号 (1-31) 的配置请求必须由交换机下游端口 (Downstream Port) 或根端口 (Root Port) 以 Unsupported Request 完成状态 (相当于 PCI 中的 Master Abort) 终止。

> **实现注意事项 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **配置空间读取副作用 (CONFIGURATION SPACE READ SIDE EFFECTS)**
>
> 在读取访问期间，除返回期望值之外发生的任何可观察的交互称为读副作用 (read side effect)。对所访问的 Function 没有特定了解的系统软件可能向 Function 配置空间内的任何位置发起读请求。强烈不希望任何此类访问产生读副作用。本规范定义的任何配置空间寄存器都不需要此类副作用。强烈建议对这些寄存器的任何实现以及任何供应商定义的配置空间寄存器都不产生读副作用。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

<!-- 📄 Page 988 -->

<a id="sec-7-3-1"></a>
## 7.3.1 Device Number § | 7.3.1 设备号 (Device Number) §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 989 -->
---

<a id="sec-7-3-1-cont"></a>
## 7.3.1 Device Number (continued) § | 7.3.1 设备号 (Device Number)（续）§


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Non-ARI Devices must capture their assigned Device Number as discussed in § Section 2.2.6.2 . Non-ARI Devices must respond to all Type 0 Configuration Read Requests, regardless of the Device Number specified in the Request.

Switches, and components wishing to incorporate more than eight Functions at their Upstream Port, are permitted to implement one or more "virtual switches" represented by multiple Type 1 Configuration Space Headers (PCI-PCI Bridge) as illustrated in § Figure 7-2. These virtual switches serve to allow fan-out beyond eight Functions. FPB provides a "flattening" mechanism that, when enabled, causes the virtual bridges of the Downstream Ports to appear in configuration space at RID addresses following the RID of the Upstream Port (see § Section 6.26 ). Since Switch Downstream Ports are permitted to appear on any Device Number, in this case all address information fields (Bus, Device, and Function Numbers) must be completely decoded to access the correct register. Any Configuration Request targeting an unimplemented Bus, Device, or Function must return a Completion with Unsupported Request Completion Status.

With an ARI Device, its Device Number is implied to be 0 rather than specified by a field within an ID. The traditional 5-bit Device Number and 3-bit Function Number fields in its associated Routing IDs, Requester IDs, and Completer IDs are interpreted as a single 8-bit Function Number. See § Section 6.13 . Any Type 0 Configuration Request targeting an unimplemented Function in an ARI Device must be handled as an Unsupported Request.

If an ARI Downstream Port has ARI Forwarding enabled, the logic that determines when to turn a Type 1 Configuration Request into a Type 0 Configuration Request no longer enforces a restriction on the traditional Device Number field being 0.

The following section provides details of the Configuration Space addressing mechanism.

PCI Express Configuration Requests use the following addressing fields:

- **Destination Segment (Flit Mode only)** - Selects one of multiple Segments that may be implemented within a Root Complex. See § Section 2.2.1.2 for a list of Segment rules.
- **Bus Number** - PCI Express maps logical PCI Bus Numbers onto PCI Express Links such that PCI-compatible configuration software views the Configuration Space of a PCI Express Hierarchy as a PCI hierarchy including multiple bus segments.
- **Device Number** - Device Number association is discussed in § Section 7.3.1 and in § Section 6.26 . When an ARI Device is targeted and the Downstream Port immediately above it is enabled for ARI Forwarding, the Device Number is implied to be 0, and the traditional Device Number field is used instead as part of an 8-bit Function Number field. See § Section 6.13 .
- **Function Number** - PCI Express also supports Multi-Function Devices using the same discovery mechanism as PCI. A Multi-Function Device must fully decode the Function Number field. A single-Function Device MUST@FLIT also fully decode the Function Number field. With ARI Devices, discovery and enumeration of Extended Functions require ARI-aware software. See § Section 6.13 .
- **Extended Register Number and Register Number** - Specify the Configuration Space address of the register being accessed (concatenated such that the Extended Register Number forms the more significant bits).

For Endpoints, the following rules apply:

- If Configuration Request Type is 1,
  - and the TLP is not an IDE TLP

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

非 ARI 设备必须捕获其所分配的设备号 (Device Number)，如 § 第 2.2.6.2 节所述。非 ARI 设备必须响应所有 Type 0 配置读请求，而与请求中指定的设备号无关。

交换机以及希望在其上游端口 (Upstream Port) 上合并超过八个 Function 的组件，允许实现一个或多个由多个 Type 1 配置空间头 (PCI-PCI 桥) 表示的"虚拟交换机 (virtual switch)"，如 § 图 7-2 所示。这些虚拟交换机用于允许扇出超过八个 Function。FPB 提供一种"扁平化 (flattening)"机制，当启用时，会使下游端口的虚拟桥在配置空间中出现在上游端口 RID 之后的 RID 地址处 (见 § 第 6.26 节)。由于允许交换机下游端口出现在任何设备号上，在这种情况下，必须完全解码所有地址信息字段 (总线、设备和 Function 号) 才能访问正确的寄存器。任何针对未实现的总线、设备或 Function 的配置请求必须返回具有 Unsupported Request 完成状态的完成报文。

对于 ARI 设备，其设备号隐含为 0，而不是由 ID 内的字段指定。其关联的路由 ID (Routing ID)、请求者 ID (Requester ID) 和完成者 ID (Completer ID) 中传统的 5 位设备号和 3 位 Function 号字段被解释为单个 8 位 Function 号。参见 § 第 6.13 节。任何针对 ARI 设备中未实现 Function 的 Type 0 配置请求都必须作为 Unsupported Request 处理。

如果 ARI 下游端口启用了 ARI 转发 (ARI Forwarding)，则在将 Type 1 配置请求转换为 Type 0 配置请求的逻辑中，不再强制要求传统设备号字段为 0。

下一节提供配置空间寻址机制的详细信息。

PCI Express 配置请求使用以下地址字段:

- **目标段 (Destination Segment, 仅 Flit 模式)** - 在根复合体内实现的多个段中选择一个。段规则列表见 § 第 2.2.1.2 节。
- **总线号 (Bus Number)** - PCI Express 将逻辑 PCI 总线号映射到 PCI Express 链路上，使得 PCI 兼容配置软件将 PCI Express 层级 (Hierarchy) 的配置空间视为包含多个总线段的 PCI 层级。
- **设备号 (Device Number)** - 设备号关联在 § 第 7.3.1 节和 § 第 6.26 节中讨论。当目标为 ARI 设备且其正上方的下游端口启用了 ARI 转发时，设备号隐含为 0，传统的设备号字段被用作 8 位 Function 号字段的一部分。参见 § 第 6.13 节。
- **Function 号 (Function Number)** - PCI Express 还支持使用与 PCI 相同的发现机制来支持多功能设备 (Multi-Function Device)。多功能设备必须完全解码 Function 号字段。单 Function 设备在 Flit 模式下 (MUST@FLIT) 也必须完全解码 Function 号字段。对于 ARI 设备，扩展 Function 的发现和枚举需要支持 ARI 的软件。参见 § 第 6.13 节。
- **扩展寄存器号 (Extended Register Number) 和寄存器号 (Register Number)** - 指定所访问寄存器的配置空间地址 (串联方式，扩展寄存器号作为更高有效位)。

对于端点 (Endpoint)，适用以下规则:

- 如果配置请求类型为 1,
  - 并且该 TLP 不是 IDE TLP

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

<a id="sec-7-3-2"></a>
## 7.3.2 Configuration Transaction Addressing | 配置事务寻址

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 7-2 Register and Register Bit-Field Types**

The following table describes the various attributes that may be applied to PCI Express Configuration register fields. The attribute indicates whether the field can be Read, Written, or updated by hardware, and over what type of reset the field's value is preserved.

[continues below]

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 7-2 寄存器及寄存器位域类型**

下表描述了可应用于 PCI Express 配置寄存器域的各种属性。属性指明该域是否可被读取、写入或由硬件更新，以及在何种类型的复位期间该域的值会被保留。

[续下表]

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**HwInit**

Hardware Initialized - Register bits are permitted, as an implementation option, to be hard-coded, initialized by system/device firmware, or initialized by hardware mechanisms such as pin strapping or nonvolatile storage. Initialization by system firmware is permitted only for system-integrated devices. Bits must be fixed in value and read-only after initialization. After Initialization, values are only permitted to change following Conventional Reset (see § Section 6.6.1 ) and subsequent re-initialization. HwInit register bits are not modified by an FLR.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**HwInit**

硬件初始化 — 作为实现选项，寄存器位可被硬编码、由系统/设备固件初始化，或由硬件机制（如引脚配置 strap 或非易失性存储）初始化。仅系统集成设备允许由系统固件初始化。初始化后，这些位的值必须固定且为只读。初始化之后，值仅允许在常规复位（参见 § 6.6.1）后及随后的重新初始化过程中变更。HwInit 寄存器位不会被 FLR 修改。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**RO**

Read-only - Register bits are read-only and cannot be altered by software. Where explicitly defined, these bits are used to reflect changing hardware state, and as a result bit values can be observed to change at run time. Register bit default values and bits that cannot change value at run time, are permitted to be hard-coded, initialized by system/device firmware, or initialized by hardware mechanisms such as pin strapping or nonvolatile storage. Initialization by system firmware is permitted only for system-integrated devices.

If the optional feature that would Set the bits is not implemented, the bits must be hardwired to Zero.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**RO**

只读 — 寄存器位为只读，软件不能更改。在明确定义的位置，这些位用于反映变化的硬件状态，因此可以在运行时观察到其值变化。寄存器位默认值以及运行时不能变化的位可被硬编码、由系统/设备固件初始化，或由硬件机制（如引脚 strap 或非易失性存储）初始化。仅系统集成设备允许由系统固件初始化。

若用于置位这些位的可选特性未实现，则这些位必须硬连线为 0。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**RW**

Read-Write - Register bits are read-write and are permitted to be either Set or Cleared by software to the desired state. If the optional feature that is associated with the bits is not implemented, the bits are permitted to be hardwired to Zero.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**RW**

读写 — 寄存器位为读写的，允许软件将其置位或清零到所需状态。若与这些位关联的可选特性未实现，则允许将这些位硬连线为 0。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**RW1C**

Write-1-to-clear status - Register bits indicate status when read. A Set bit indicates a status event which is Cleared by writing a 1b. Writing a 0b to RW1C bits has no effect.

If the optional feature that would Set the bit is not implemented, the bit must be read-only and hardwired to Zero.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**RW1C**

写 1 清零状态位 — 寄存器位在读取时表示状态。置位的位表示状态事件，通过写入 1b 来清除。向 RW1C 位写入 0b 无效。

若用于置位该位的可选特性未实现，则该位必须为只读并硬连线为 0。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

<a id="sec-7-3-3"></a>
## 7.3.3 Configuration Request Routing Rules | 配置请求路由规则


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

and it is targeting a Device's captured Bus Number (See § Section 9.2.1.2 for SR-IOV devices that consume multiple Bus numbers)

  - Follow the rules for handling Unsupported Requests

- If Configuration Request Type is 0, or if Configuration Request Type is 1 and the TLP is an IDE TLP associated with a Selective IDE Stream in the Secure state,

  - Determine if the Request addresses a valid local Configuration Space of an implemented Function
    - If so, process the Request
    - If not, follow rules for handling Unsupported Requests

For Root Ports, Switches, and PCI Express-PCI Bridges, the following rules apply:

- Propagation of Configuration Requests from Downstream to Upstream as well as peer-to-peer are not supported

  - Configuration Requests are initiated only by the Host Bridge, including those passed through the SFI CAM mechanism

- If Configuration Request Type is 0,

  - Determine if the Request addresses a valid local Configuration Space of an implemented Function
    - If so, process the Request
    - If not, follow rules for handling Unsupported Requests

- If Configuration Request Type is 1, apply the following tests, in sequence, to the Bus Number and Device Number fields:

  - If in the case of a PCI Express-PCI Bridge, equal to the Bus Number assigned to secondary PCI bus or, in the case of a Switch or Root Complex, equal to the Bus Number and decoded Device Numbers assigned to one of the Root (Root Complex) or Downstream Ports (Switch), or if required based on the FPB Routing ID mechanism,

    - Transform the Request to Type 0 by changing the value in the Type[4:0] field of the Request (see § Table 2-3) - all other fields of the Request remain unchanged
    - Forward the Request to that Downstream Port (or PCI bus, in the case of a PCI Express-PCI Bridge)

  - If not equal to the Bus Number of any of Downstream Ports or secondary PCI bus, but in the range of Bus Numbers assigned to either a Downstream Port or a secondary PCI bus, or if required based on the FPB Routing ID mechanism,

    - Forward the Request to that Downstream Port interface without modification

  - Else (none of the above)

    - The Request is invalid - follow the rules for handling Unsupported Requests

- PCI Express-PCI Bridges must terminate as Unsupported Requests any Configuration Requests for which the Extended Register Address field is non-zero that are directed towards a PCI bus that does not support Extended Configuration Space.

Note: This type of access is a consequence of a programming error.

Additional rule specific to Root Complexes:

- Configuration Requests addressing a Destination Segment and Bus Numbers assigned to devices within the Root Complex are processed by the Root Complex

  - The assignment of Segment Numbers and Bus Numbers to the devices within a Root Complex may be done in an implementation specific way.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

并且它针对的是设备已捕获的 Bus Number（关于占用多个 Bus 号的 SR-IOV 设备，参见 § 9.2.1.2）

  - 遵循处理不支持请求（Unsupported Requests）的规则

- 如果配置请求的类型为 0，或者配置请求的类型为 1 且该 TLP 是与处于 Secure 状态的 Selective IDE Stream 关联的 IDE TLP，

  - 判断该请求是否寻址到已实现功能（Function）的有效本地配置空间（Configuration Space）
    - 如果是，则处理该请求
    - 如果不是，则遵循处理不支持请求的规则

对于根端口（Root Ports）、交换机（Switches）和 PCI Express-PCI 桥（PCI Express-PCI Bridges），适用以下规则：

- 不支持将配置请求从下游（Downstream）传播到上游（Upstream），也不支持点对点传播

  - 配置请求仅由主桥（Host Bridge）发起，包括通过 SFI CAM 机制传递的请求

- 如果配置请求的类型为 0，

  - 判断该请求是否寻址到已实现功能的有效本地配置空间
    - 如果是，则处理该请求
    - 如果不是，则遵循处理不支持请求的规则

- 如果配置请求的类型为 1，则按顺序对 Bus Number 和 Device Number 字段应用以下测试：

  - 如果在 PCI Express-PCI 桥的情况下，等于分配给 secondary PCI 总线的 Bus Number；或者在交换机（Switch）或根复合体（Root Complex）的情况下，等于分配给某个根端口（根复合体）或下游端口（交换机）的 Bus Number 及已解码的 Device Number；或者根据 FPB Routing ID 机制所需，

    - 通过修改请求的 Type[4:0] 字段（参见 § 表 2-3）将请求转换为 Type 0 — 请求的其他字段保持不变
    - 将请求转发到该下游端口（或在 PCI Express-PCI 桥的情况下为 PCI 总线）

  - 如果不等于任何下游端口或 secondary PCI 总线的 Bus Number，但处于分配给某个下游端口或 secondary PCI 总线的 Bus Number 范围内；或者根据 FPB Routing ID 机制所需，

    - 不做修改地将请求转发到该下游端口接口

  - 否则（不符合上述任何情况）

    - 该请求无效 — 遵循处理不支持请求的规则

- 对于 Extended Register Address 字段非零且指向不支持扩展配置空间（Extended Configuration Space）的 PCI 总线的配置请求，PCI Express-PCI 桥必须将其作为不支持请求终止。

注：此类访问通常是编程错误所致。

根复合体（Root Complex）的特定附加规则：

- 寻址到根复合体内设备所分配的目标段（Destination Segment）和 Bus Number 的配置请求由根复合体处理

  - 根复合体内设备的段号（Segment Number）和 Bus Number 分配可以采用实现特定的方式。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<!-- 📄 Page 990 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For all types of devices:

Configuration Reads and Writes to unimplemented registers are not considered to be errors. Unless errors defined elsewhere in this specification are detected and need to be reported, such Requests must return a Completion with Successful Completion status, with reads returning a data value of all 0's and writes discarding the write data without effect.

All other Configuration Space addressing fields are decoded as described elsewhere in this specification.

PCI Special Cycles (see the [PCI] for details) are not directly supported by PCI Express. PCI Special Cycles may be directed to PCI bus segments behind PCI Express-PCI Bridges using Type 1 configuration cycles as described in the [PCI].

Configuration register fields are assigned one of the attributes described in § Table 7-2. All PCI Express components, with the exception of the Root Complex and system-integrated devices, initialize register fields to specified default values. Root Complexes and system-integrated devices initialize register fields as required by the firmware for a particular system implementation.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对所有类型的设备：

对未实现寄存器的配置读和写不被视为错误。除非本规范其他位置定义的错误被检测到且需要报告，否则此类请求必须返回带有 Successful Completion 状态（成功完成状态）的完成报文（Completion），其中读取返回全 0 的数据值，写入则丢弃写入数据而不生效。

所有其他配置空间寻址字段按本规范其他位置的描述进行解码。

PCI Express 不直接支持 PCI Special Cycles（PCI 特殊周期，详见 [PCI]）。可以使用 [PCI] 中描述的 Type 1 配置周期，将 PCI Special Cycles 定向到 PCI Express-PCI 桥后面的 PCI 总线段。

配置寄存器字段被赋予 § 表 7-2 中描述的属性之一。除根复合体和系统集成设备外，所有 PCI Express 组件都按指定的默认值初始化寄存器字段。根复合体和系统集成设备根据特定系统实现的固件要求初始化寄存器字段。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 7-2 Register and Register Bit-Field Types**

| Register Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| HwInit | Hardware Initialized - Register bits are permitted, as an implementation option, to be hard-coded, initialized by system/device firmware, or initialized by hardware mechanisms such as pin strapping or nonvolatile storage. Initialization by system firmware is permitted only for system-integrated devices. Bits must be fixed in value and read-only after initialization. After Initialization, values are only permitted to change following Conventional Reset (see § Section 6.6.1 ) and subsequent re-initialization. HwInit register bits are not modified by an FLR. |
| RO | Read-only - Register bits are read-only and cannot be altered by software. Where explicitly defined, these bits are used to reflect changing hardware state, and as a result bit values can be observed to change at run time. Register bit default values and bits that cannot change value at run time, are permitted to be hard-coded, initialized by system/device firmware, or initialized by hardware mechanisms such as pin strapping or nonvolatile storage. Initialization by system firmware is permitted only for system-integrated devices.<br><br>If the optional feature that would Set the bits is not implemented, the bits must be hardwired to Zero. |
| RW | Read-Write - Register bits are read-write and are permitted to be either Set or Cleared by software to the desired state. If the optional feature that is associated with the bits is not implemented, the bits are permitted to be hardwired to Zero. |
| RW1C | Write-1-to-clear status - Register bits indicate status when read. A Set bit indicates a status event which is Cleared by writing a 1b. Writing a 0b to RW1C bits has no effect.<br><br>If the optional feature that would Set the bit is not implemented, the bit must be read-only and hardwired to Zero. |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 7-2 寄存器及寄存器位域类型**

| 寄存器属性 | 描述 |
|---|---|
| HwInit | 硬件初始化 — 作为实现选项，寄存器位可被硬编码、由系统/设备固件初始化，或由硬件机制（如引脚 strap 或非易失性存储）初始化。仅系统集成设备允许由系统固件初始化。初始化后，这些位的值必须固定且为只读。初始化之后，值仅允许在常规复位（参见 § 6.6.1）后及随后的重新初始化过程中变更。HwInit 寄存器位不会被 FLR 修改。 |
| RO | 只读 — 寄存器位为只读，软件不能更改。在明确定义的位置，这些位用于反映变化的硬件状态，因此可以在运行时观察到其值变化。寄存器位默认值以及运行时不能变化的位可被硬编码、由系统/设备固件初始化，或由硬件机制（如引脚 strap 或非易失性存储）初始化。仅系统集成设备允许由系统固件初始化。<br><br>若用于置位这些位的可选特性未实现，则这些位必须硬连线为 0。 |
| RW | 读写 — 寄存器位为读写的，允许软件将其置位或清零到所需状态。若与这些位关联的可选特性未实现，则允许将这些位硬连线为 0。 |
| RW1C | 写 1 清零状态位 — 寄存器位在读取时表示状态。置位的位表示状态事件，通过写入 1b 来清除。向 RW1C 位写入 0b 无效。<br><br>若用于置位该位的可选特性未实现，则该位必须为只读并硬连线为 0。 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<a id="sec-7-3-4"></a>
## 7.3.4 PCI Special Cycles | PCI 特殊周期

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

PCI Special Cycles are not supported by PCI Express. Refer to § Section 7.3.3 for additional details.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

PCI Express 不支持 PCI Special Cycles。更多详细信息请参阅 § 7.3.3。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-7-4"></a>
## 7.4 Configuration Register Types | 配置寄存器类型

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Configuration register fields are assigned one of the attributes described in § Table 7-2. All PCI Express components, with the exception of the Root Complex and system-integrated devices, initialize register fields to specified default values. Root Complexes and system-integrated devices initialize register fields as required by the firmware for a particular system implementation.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

配置寄存器字段被赋予 § 表 7-2 中描述的属性之一。除根复合体和系统集成设备外，所有 PCI Express 组件都按指定的默认值初始化寄存器字段。根复合体和系统集成设备根据特定系统实现的固件要求初始化寄存器字段。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 991 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

| Register Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| ROS | Sticky - Read-only - Register bits are read-only and cannot be altered by software. If the optional feature that would Set the bit is not implemented, the bit is hardwired to Zero. Bits are neither initialized nor modified by hot reset or FLR. Where noted, devices that consume auxiliary power must preserve sticky register bit values when auxiliary power consumption (via either Aux Power PM Enable or PME_En) is enabled. In these cases, register bits are neither initialized nor modified by Hot, Warm, or Cold Reset (see § Section 6.6 ). |
| RWS | Sticky - Read-Write - Register bits are read-write and are Set or Cleared by software to the desired state. Bits are neither initialized nor modified by hot reset or FLR.<br><br>If the optional feature that is associated with the bits is not implemented, the bits are permitted to be hardwired to Zero.<br><br>Where noted, devices that consume auxiliary power must preserve sticky register bit values when auxiliary power consumption (via either Aux Power PM Enable or PME_En) is enabled. In these cases, register bits are neither initialized nor modified by Hot, Warm, or Cold Reset (see § Section 6.6 ). |
| RW1CS | Sticky - Write-1-to-clear status - Register bits indicate status when read. A Set bit indicates a status event which is Cleared by writing a 1b. Writing a 0b to RW1CS bits has no effect. If the optional feature that would Set the bit is not implemented, the bit is read-only and hardwired to Zero. Bits are neither initialized nor modified by hot reset or FLR.<br><br>Where noted, devices that consume auxiliary power must preserve sticky register bit values when auxiliary power consumption (via either Aux Power PM Enable or PME_En) is enabled. In these cases, register bits are neither initialized nor modified by Hot, Warm, or Cold Reset (see § Section 6.6 ). |
| RsvdP | Reserved and Preserved - Reserved for future RW implementations. Register bits are read-only and must return zero when read. Software must preserve the value read for writes to bits. |
| RsvdZ | Reserved and Zero - Reserved for future RW1C implementations. Register bits are read-only and must return zero when read. Software must use 0b for writes to bits. |

For SR-IOV devices, many registers or fields in VFs are required to be reserved or hardwired to Zero. Before the Single Root I/O Virtualization and Sharing (SR-IOV) Specification was merged into the PCI Express Base Specification, the SR-IOV specification contained many tables summarizing requirements differences for PFs and VFs, relative to the Base specification. These tables contained dedicated columns for PF attributes and VF attributes, though there were relatively few differences for PF attributes.

To provide a clear, consolidated, and concise indication of PF and VF attribute differences from other Function types, this specification eliminated most of those tables. Instead, special field types from the following table indicate VF attribute differences, and any additional attribute or semantic differences with PFs and VFs are covered within the register description column or elsewhere.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

| 寄存器属性 | 描述 |
|---|---|
| ROS | Sticky（粘性）— 只读 — 寄存器位为只读，软件不能更改。若用于置位该位的可选特性未实现，则该位硬连线为 0。这些位既不会被热复位（Hot Reset）初始化或修改，也不会被 FLR 修改。如有注明，消耗辅助电源的设备在启用辅助电源消耗（通过 Aux Power PM Enable 或 PME_En）时必须保留粘性寄存器位的值。在这种情况下，寄存器位既不会被热复位（Hot Reset）、温复位（Warm Reset）也不会被冷复位（Cold Reset）初始化或修改（参见 § 6.6）。 |
| RWS | Sticky（粘性）— 读写 — 寄存器位为读写的，可由软件置位或清零到所需状态。这些位既不会被热复位初始化或修改，也不会被 FLR 修改。<br><br>若与这些位关联的可选特性未实现，则允许将这些位硬连线为 0。<br><br>如有注明，消耗辅助电源的设备在启用辅助电源消耗（通过 Aux Power PM Enable 或 PME_En）时必须保留粘性寄存器位的值。在这种情况下，寄存器位既不会被热复位、温复位也不会被冷复位初始化或修改（参见 § 6.6）。 |
| RW1CS | Sticky（粘性）— 写 1 清零状态位 — 寄存器位在读取时表示状态。置位的位表示状态事件，通过写入 1b 来清除。向 RW1CS 位写入 0b 无效。若用于置位该位的可选特性未实现，则该位为只读并硬连线为 0。这些位既不会被热复位初始化或修改，也不会被 FLR 修改。<br><br>如有注明，消耗辅助电源的设备在启用辅助电源消耗（通过 Aux Power PM Enable 或 PME_En）时必须保留粘性寄存器位的值。在这种情况下，寄存器位既不会被热复位、温复位也不会被冷复位初始化或修改（参见 § 6.6）。 |
| RsvdP | 保留并保持 — 为未来的 RW 实现保留。寄存器位为只读，读取时必须返回零。软件必须在写入这些位时保留读取的值。 |
| RsvdZ | 保留并为零 — 为未来的 RW1C 实现保留。寄存器位为只读，读取时必须返回零。软件必须对写入使用 0b。 |

对于 SR-IOV 设备，VF 中的许多寄存器或域要求被保留或硬连线为 0。在 Single Root I/O Virtualization and Sharing（SR-IOV，单根 I/O 虚拟化）规范合并到 PCI Express 基础规范之前，SR-IOV 规范包含许多表，汇总了相对于基础规范 PF 和 VF 的要求差异。这些表为 PF 属性和 VF 属性设置了专用的列，尽管 PF 属性的差异相对较少。

为了清晰、合并且简洁地指示 PF 和 VF 与其他功能类型之间的属性差异，本规范删除了大部分此类表格。取而代之，下表中的特殊字段类型指示 VF 的属性差异，与 PF 和 VF 的任何其他属性或语义差异在寄存器描述列或其他位置中说明。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-7-5"></a>
## 7.5 PCI and PCIe Capabilities Required by the Base Spec for all Ports | 基础规范要求所有端口支持的 PCI 和 PCIe 能力

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

This section describes the PCI and PCI Express capabilities required by the Base Specification for all Ports.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

本节介绍基础规范要求所有端口支持的 PCI 和 PCI Express 能力。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-7-5-1"></a>
## 7.5.1 PCI-Compatible Configuration Registers | PCI 兼容配置寄存器


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The first 256 bytes of a Function's Configuration Space form the PCI-compatible region. This region completely aliases the conventional PCI Configuration Space of the Function. Legacy PCI devices can also be accessed with the ECAM without requiring any modifications to the device hardware or device driver software.

Layout of the Configuration Space and format of individual configuration registers are depicted following the little-endian convention.

§ Figure 7-4 details allocation for common register fields of Type 0 and Type 1 Configuration Space Headers for PCI Express Device Functions. Fields labeled Type Specific vary between different Configuration Space header types.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

功能配置空间（Configuration Space）的前 256 字节构成 PCI 兼容区域（PCI-Compatible region）。该区域完整映射了该功能的传统 PCI 配置空间。传统 PCI 设备也可以使用 ECAM 访问，无需修改设备硬件或设备驱动程序软件。

配置空间的布局和各个配置寄存器的格式按小端字节序（little-endian）约定进行描述。

§ 图 7-4 详细说明了 PCI Express 设备功能的 Type 0 和 Type 1 配置空间头（Configuration Space Header）的公共寄存器字段分配。标记为 Type Specific（类型相关）的字段在不同的配置空间头类型之间有所不同。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


>>> **Figure 7-4.** Common Configuration Space Header
>>> <img src="figures/chapter_07/fig_0993_1.png" width="700">

<a id="sec-7-5-1-1"></a>
## 7.5.1.1 Type 0/1 Common Configuration Space | Type 0/1 公共配置空间

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

These registers are defined for both Type 0 and Type 1 Configuration Space Headers. The PCI Express-specific interpretation of these registers is defined in this section.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

这些寄存器为 Type 0 和 Type 1 配置空间头共同定义。这些寄存器的 PCI Express 特定解释在本节中定义。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

>>> **Figure 7-4.** Common Configuration Space Header (公共配置空间头)
>>> <img src="figures/chapter_07/fig_0994_1_tight.png" width="700">

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-7-5-1-1-1"></a>
## 7.5.1.1.1 Vendor ID Register (Offset 00h) | Vendor ID 寄存器（偏移 00h）


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For non-VFs, the Vendor ID register is HwInit and the value in this register identifies the manufacturer of the Function. In keeping with PCI-SIG procedures, valid vendor identifiers must be allocated by the PCI-SIG to ensure uniqueness. Each vendor must have at least one Vendor ID. It is recommended that software read the Vendor ID register to determine if a Function is present, where a value of FFFFh indicates that no Function is present.

For VFs, this field must return FFFFh when read. VI software should return the Vendor ID value from the associated PF as the Vendor ID value for the VF.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于非 VF，Vendor ID 寄存器为 HwInit，其值标识该功能的制造商。根据 PCI-SIG 的流程，有效的供应商标识符必须由 PCI-SIG 分配以确保唯一性。每个供应商必须至少拥有一个 Vendor ID。建议软件读取 Vendor ID 寄存器以确定功能是否存在，其中 FFFFh 的值表示不存在功能。

对于 VF，读取该字段时必须返回 FFFFh。VI 软件应返回来自关联 PF 的 Vendor ID 值作为 VF 的 Vendor ID 值。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<!-- 📄 Page 995 -->
---

<a id="sec-7-5-1-1-2"></a>
## 7.5.1.1.2 Device ID Register (Offset 02h) | Device ID 寄存器（偏移 02h）

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For non-VFs, the Device ID register is HwInit and the value in this register identifies the particular Function. The Device ID must be allocated by the vendor. The Device ID, in conjunction with the Vendor ID and Revision ID, are used as one mechanism for software to determine which driver should be loaded. The vendor must ensure that the chosen values do not result in the use of an incompatible device driver.

For VFs, this field must return FFFFh when read. VI software should return the VF Device ID (see § Section 9.3.3.11 ) value from the associated PF as the Device ID for the VF.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于非 VF，Device ID 寄存器为 HwInit，其值标识特定的功能。Device ID 必须由供应商分配。Device ID 与 Vendor ID 和 Revision ID 一起用作软件决定加载哪个驱动程序的机制之一。供应商必须确保所选值不会导致使用不兼容的设备驱动程序。

对于 VF，读取该字段时必须返回 FFFFh。VI 软件应返回来自关联 PF 的 VF Device ID 值（参见 § 9.3.3.11）作为 VF 的 Device ID。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: LEGACY PCI PROBING SOFTWARE**

Returning FFFFh for Device ID and Vendor ID values allows some legacy software to ignore VFs. See § Section 7.5.1.1.1 .

**实现说明：传统 PCI 探测软件**

为 Device ID 和 Vendor ID 返回 FFFFh 允许某些传统软件忽略 VF。参见 § 7.5.1.1.1。

<a id="sec-7-5-1-1-3"></a>
## 7.5.1.1.3 Command Register (Offset 04h) | Command 寄存器（偏移 04h）

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

§ Table 7-4 defines the Command Register and the layout of the register is shown in § Figure 7-5. Individual bits in the Command Register may or may not be implemented depending on the feature set supported by the Function. For PCI Express to PCI/PCI-X Bridges, refer to the [PCIe-to-PCI-PCI-X-Bridge] for requirements for this register.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

§ 表 7-4 定义了 Command 寄存器，其布局如 § 图 7-5 所示。Command 寄存器中的各个位是否实现取决于该功能支持的特性集。对于 PCI Express 到 PCI/PCI-X 桥，请参阅 [PCIe-to-PCI-PCI-X-Bridge] 以了解此寄存器的要求。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

>>> **Figure 7-5.** Command Register (Command 寄存器)
>>> <img src="figures/chapter_07/fig_0995_1_tight.png" width="700">

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 7-4 Command Register**

| Bit Location | Register Description | Attributes |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | I/O Space Enable - Controls a Function's response to I/O Space accesses. When this bit is Clear, all received I/O accesses are caused to be handled as Unsupported Requests. When this bit is Set, the Function is enabled to decode the address and further process I/O Space accesses. For a Function with a Type 1 Configuration Space Header, this bit controls the response to I/O Space accesses received on its Primary Side.<br><br>Default value of this bit is 0b.<br><br>This bit is permitted to be hardwired to Zero if a Function does not support I/O Space accesses.<br><br>This bit does not apply to VFs and must be hardwired to Zero. | RW<br>VF ROZ |
| 1 | Memory Space Enable - Controls a Function's response to Memory Space accesses. When this bit is Clear, all received Memory Space accesses are caused to be handled as Unsupported Requests. When this bit is Set, the Function is enabled to decode the address and further process Memory Space accesses. For a Function with a Type 1 Configuration Space Header, this bit controls the response to Memory Space accesses received on its Primary Side.<br><br>Default value of this bit is 0b.<br><br>This bit is permitted to be hardwired to 0b if a Function does not support Memory Space accesses.<br><br>This bit does not apply to VFs and must be hardwired to Zero. VF Memory Space is controlled by the VF MSE bit in the SR-IOV Control Register. | RW<br>VF ROZ |
| 2 | Bus Master Enable - Controls the ability of a Function to issue Memory and I/O Read/Write Requests, and the ability of a Port to forward Memory and I/O Read/Write Requests in the Upstream direction<br><br>• Functions with a Type 0 Configuration Space Header:<br>When this bit is Set, the Function is allowed to issue Memory or I/O Requests.<br>When this bit is Clear, the Function is not allowed to issue any Memory or I/O Requests.<br>Note that as MSI/MSI-X interrupt Messages are in-band memory writes, setting the Bus Master Enable bit to 0b disables MSI/MSI-X interrupt Messages as well.<br>Transactions for a VF that has its Bus Master Enable Set must not be blocked by transactions for VFs that have their Bus Master Enable Cleared.<br>Requests other than Memory or I/O Requests are not controlled by this bit.<br>Default value of this bit is 0b.<br>This bit is hardwired to 0b if a Function does not generate Memory or I/O Requests.<br>• Functions with a Type 1 Configurations Space Header:<br>This bit controls the initiating of and the forwarding of Memory or I/O Requests by a Port in the Upstream direction. When this bit is 0b, Memory and I/O Requests received at a Root Port or the Downstream side of a Switch Port must be handled as Unsupported Requests (UR), and for Non-Posted Requests a Completion with UR Completion Status must be returned. This bit does not affect forwarding of Completions in either the Upstream or Downstream direction.<br>The forwarding of Requests other than Memory or I/O Requests is not controlled by this bit.<br>Default value of this bit is 0b. | RW |
| 3 | Special Cycle Enable - This bit was originally described in the [PCI]. Its functionality does not apply to PCI Express and the bit must be hardwired to 0b. | RO |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 7-4 Command 寄存器**

| 位位置 | 寄存器描述 | 属性 |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | I/O Space Enable（I/O 空间使能）— 控制功能对 I/O Space 访问的响应。当该位清零时，所有收到的 I/O 访问都将作为不支持请求处理。当该位置位时，功能被使能以解码地址并进一步处理 I/O Space 访问。对于 Type 1 配置空间头的功能，该位控制其 Primary 侧收到的 I/O Space 访问的响应。<br><br>此位的默认值为 0b。<br><br>如果功能不支持 I/O Space 访问，则允许将该位硬连线为 0。<br><br>该位不适用于 VF，必须硬连线为 0。 | RW<br>VF ROZ |
| 1 | Memory Space Enable（内存空间使能）— 控制功能对 Memory Space 访问的响应。当该位清零时，所有收到的 Memory Space 访问都将作为不支持请求处理。当该位置位时，功能被使能以解码地址并进一步处理 Memory Space 访问。对于 Type 1 配置空间头的功能，该位控制其 Primary 侧收到的 Memory Space 访问的响应。<br><br>此位的默认值为 0b。<br><br>如果功能不支持 Memory Space 访问，则允许将该位硬连线为 0b。<br><br>该位不适用于 VF，必须硬连线为 0。VF 内存空间由 SR-IOV Control 寄存器中的 VF MSE 位控制。 | RW<br>VF ROZ |
| 2 | Bus Master Enable（总线主控使能）— 控制功能发出 Memory 和 I/O 读/写请求的能力，以及端口在上游方向转发 Memory 和 I/O 读/写请求的能力<br><br>• Type 0 配置空间头的功能：<br>当该位置位时，允许该功能发出 Memory 或 I/O 请求。<br>当该位清零时，不允许该功能发出任何 Memory 或 I/O 请求。<br>请注意，由于 MSI/MSI-X 中断消息是带内内存写操作，将 Bus Master Enable 位置为 0b 也会禁用 MSI/MSI-X 中断消息。<br>Bus Master Enable 已置位的 VF 的事务不得被 Bus Master Enable 已清零的 VF 的事务阻塞。<br>Memory 或 I/O 请求以外的请求不受该位控制。<br>此位的默认值为 0b。<br>如果功能不生成 Memory 或 I/O 请求，则该位硬连线为 0b。<br>• Type 1 配置空间头的功能：<br>该位控制端口在上游方向发起和转发 Memory 或 I/O 请求。当该位为 0b 时，在根端口或交换机端口下游侧收到的 Memory 和 I/O 请求必须作为不支持请求（UR）处理，对于 Non-Posted 请求，必须返回带有 UR 完成状态的完成报文。该位不影响完成报文在上游或下游方向的转发。<br>Memory 或 I/O 请求以外的请求的转发不受该位控制。<br>此位的默认值为 0b。 | RW |
| 3 | Special Cycle Enable（特殊周期使能）— 该位最初在 [PCI] 中描述。其功能不适用于 PCI Express，该位必须硬连线为 0b。 | RO |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<!-- 📄 Page 997 -->

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

| Bit Location | Register Description | Attributes |
|---|---|---|
| 4 | Memory Write and Invalidate - This bit was originally described in the [PCI] and the [PCI-to-PCI-Bridge]. Its functionality does not apply to PCI Express and the bit must be hardwired to 0b. For PCI Express to PCI/PCI-X Bridges, refer to the [PCIe-to-PCI-PCI-X-Bridge] for requirements for this register. | RO |
| 5 | VGA Palette Snoop - This bit was originally described in the [PCI] and the [PCI-to-PCI-Bridge]. Its functionality does not apply to PCI Express and the bit must be hardwired to 0b. | RO |
| 6 | Parity Error Response - See § Section 7.5.1.1.14 .<br>This bit controls the logging of poisoned TLPs in the Master Data Parity Error bit in the Status Register.<br>An RCiEP that is not associated with a Root Complex Event Collector is permitted to hardwire this bit to 0b.<br>Default value of this bit is 0b. | RW<br>VF RsvdP |
| 7 | IDSEL Stepping/Wait Cycle Control - This bit was originally described in the [PCI]. Its functionality does not apply to PCI Express and the bit must be hardwired to 0b. | RO |
| 8 | SERR# Enable - See § Section 7.5.1.1.14 .<br>When Set, this bit enables reporting upstream of Non-fatal and Fatal errors detected by the Function.<br>Note that errors are reported if enabled either through this bit or through the PCI Express specific bits in the Device Control Register (see § Section 7.5.3.4 ).<br>In addition, for Functions with Type 1 Configuration Space Headers, this bit controls transmission by the primary interface of ERR_NONFATAL and ERR_FATAL error Messages forwarded from the secondary interface. This bit does not affect the transmission of forwarded ERR_COR messages.<br>An RCiEP that is not associated with a Root Complex Event Collector is permitted to hardwire this bit to 0b.<br>Default value of this bit is 0b. | RW<br>VF RsvdP |
| 9 | Fast Back-to-Back Transactions Enable - This bit was originally described in the [PCI]. Its functionality does not apply to PCI Express and the bit must be hardwired to 0b. | RO |
| 10 | Interrupt Disable - Controls the ability of a Function to generate INTx emulation interrupts. When Set, Functions are prevented from asserting INTx interrupts.<br>Any INTx emulation interrupts already asserted by the Function must be deasserted when this bit is Set.<br>As described in § Section 2.2.8.1 , INTx interrupts use virtual wires that must, if asserted, be deasserted using the appropriate Deassert_INTx message(s) when this bit is Set.<br>Only the INTx virtual wire interrupt(s) associated with the Function(s) for which this bit is Set are affected.<br>For Functions with a Type 0 Configuration Space Header that generate INTx interrupts, this bit is required. For Functions with a Type 0 Configuration Space Header that do not generate INTx interrupts, this bit is optional. If not implemented, this bit must be hardwired to 0b.<br>For Functions with a Type 1 Configuration Space Header that generate INTx interrupts on their own behalf, this bit is required. This bit has no effect on interrupts forwarded from the secondary side.<br>For Functions with a Type 1 Configuration Space Header that do not generate INTx interrupts on their own behalf this bit is optional. If not implemented, this bit must be hardwired to 0b.<br>Default value of this bit is 0b.<br>This bit does not apply to VFs and must be hardwired to Zero. | RW<br>VF ROZ |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

| 位位置 | 寄存器描述 | 属性 |
|---|---|---|
| 4 | Memory Write and Invalidate（内存写入无效）— 该位最初在 [PCI] 和 [PCI-to-PCI-Bridge] 中描述。其功能不适用于 PCI Express，该位必须硬连线为 0b。对于 PCI Express 到 PCI/PCI-X 桥，请参阅 [PCIe-to-PCI-PCI-X-Bridge] 以了解此寄存器的要求。 | RO |
| 5 | VGA Palette Snoop（VGA 调色板监听）— 该位最初在 [PCI] 和 [PCI-to-PCI-Bridge] 中描述。其功能不适用于 PCI Express，该位必须硬连线为 0b。 | RO |
| 6 | Parity Error Response（奇偶校验错误响应）— 参见 § 7.5.1.1.14。<br>该位控制将中毒 TLP（poisoned TLPs）记录到 Status 寄存器中的 Master Data Parity Error 位。<br>未与根复合体事件收集器（Root Complex Event Collector）关联的 RCiEP 允许将该位硬连线为 0b。<br>此位的默认值为 0b。 | RW<br>VF RsvdP |
| 7 | IDSEL Stepping/Wait Cycle Control（IDSEL 步进/等待周期控制）— 该位最初在 [PCI] 中描述。其功能不适用于 PCI Express，该位必须硬连线为 0b。 | RO |
| 8 | SERR# Enable（SERR# 使能）— 参见 § 7.5.1.1.14。<br>当置位时，该位使能该功能检测到的非致命错误和致命错误向上游报告。<br>请注意，无论通过此位还是通过 Device Control 寄存器（参见 § 7.5.3.4）中的 PCI Express 特定位启用，都会报告错误。<br>此外，对于 Type 1 配置空间头的功能，该位控制 primary 接口从 secondary 接口转发的 ERR_NONFATAL 和 ERR_FATAL 错误消息的传输。该位不影响转发的 ERR_COR 消息的传输。<br>未与根复合体事件收集器关联的 RCiEP 允许将该位硬连线为 0b。<br>此位的默认值为 0b。 | RW<br>VF RsvdP |
| 9 | Fast Back-to-Back Transactions Enable（快速背靠背事务使能）— 该位最初在 [PCI] 中描述。其功能不适用于 PCI Express，该位必须硬连线为 0b。 | RO |
| 10 | Interrupt Disable（中断禁用）— 控制功能生成 INTx 仿真中断的能力。当置位时，禁止功能断言 INTx 中断。<br>当该位置位时，功能已断言的所有 INTx 仿真中断必须撤销。<br>如 § 2.2.8.1 所述，INTx 中断使用虚拟线，如果已断言，则当该位置位时必须使用适当的 Deassert_INTx 消息撤销断言。<br>仅与该位置位的功能关联的 INTx 虚拟线中断受影响。<br>对于生成 INTx 中断的 Type 0 配置空间头的功能，该位是必需的。对于不生成 INTx 中断的 Type 0 配置空间头的功能，该位是可选的。如果未实现，该位必须硬连线为 0b。<br>对于代表自身生成 INTx 中断的 Type 1 配置空间头的功能，该位是必需的。该位对从 secondary 侧转发的中断没有影响。<br>对于不代表自身生成 INTx 中断的 Type 1 配置空间头的功能，该位是可选的。如果未实现，该位必须硬连线为 0b。<br>此位的默认值为 0b。<br>该位不适用于 VF，必须硬连线为 0。 | RW<br>VF ROZ |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-7-5-1-1-4-2"></a>
## 7.5.1.1.4 Status Register (Offset 06h) | Status 寄存器（偏移 06h）

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

§ Table 7-5 defines the Status Register and the layout of the register is shown in § Figure 7-6. Functions may not need to implement all bits, depending on the feature set supported by the Function. For PCI Express to PCI/PCI-X Bridges, refer to the [PCIe-to-PCI-PCI-X-Bridge] for requirements for this register.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

§ 表 7-5 定义了 Status 寄存器，其布局如 § 图 7-6 所示。功能可能不需要实现所有位，这取决于该功能支持的特性集。对于 PCI Express 到 PCI/PCI-X 桥，请参阅 [PCIe-to-PCI-PCI-X-Bridge] 以了解此寄存器的要求。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

>>> **Figure 7-6.** Status Register (Status 寄存器)
>>> <img src="figures/chapter_07/fig_0998_1_tight.png" width="700">

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🏆 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 7-5 Status Register**

| Bit Location | Register Description | Attributes |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Immediate Readiness - This optional bit, when Set, indicates the Function is guaranteed to be ready to successfully complete valid Configuration Requests at any time. It is permitted for this indication to be based on implementation specific knowledge of how long it takes the host to become ready to issue Configuration Requests.<br>When this bit is Set, for accesses to this Function, software is exempt from all requirements to delay configuration accesses following any type of reset, including but not limited to the timing requirements defined in § Section 6.6 .<br>How this guarantee is established is beyond the scope of this document.<br>It is permitted that system software/firmware provide mechanisms that supersede the indication provided by this bit, however such software/firmware mechanisms are outside the scope of this specification. | RO |
| 3 | Interrupt Status - When Set, indicates that an INTx emulation interrupt is pending internally in the Function. | RO<br>VF ROZ |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 7-5 Status 寄存器**

| 位位置 | 寄存器描述 | 属性 |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Immediate Readiness（即时就绪）— 该可选位置位时，表示保证该功能随时准备好成功完成有效的配置请求。允许该指示基于关于主机需要多长时间才能准备好发出配置请求的实现特定知识。<br>当该位置位时，对于访问此功能，软件免于在任何类型的复位后延迟配置访问的所有要求，包括但不限于 § 6.6 中定义的时序要求。<br>如何建立此保证不在本文档的范围内。<br>允许系统软件/固件提供可替代此位指示的机制，但此类软件/固件机制不在本规范的范围内。 | RO |
| 3 | Interrupt Status（中断状态）— 当置位时，表示功能内部有 INTx 仿真中断挂起。 | RO<br>VF ROZ |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)
